name: IB Biology
questions: Where do intermolecular forces shape what biology can do?
Which biological processes depend on being at an interface?
What does dispersing offspring gain. And what does it cost?
How has the ability to move shaped what evolution selects for?
How do the properties of individual parts shape biological systems?
Where is light essential for life. And where is it not?
When does specificity beat versatility, and vice versa?
How do different nutrition modes differ in inputs, processes, and outputs?
Why do biologists rely on models even when they're simplifications?
What sets the upper limit on biological capacity?
What does being small enable that being large doesn't, and vice versa?
What turns identical cells into specialized ones?
How do large organisms get materials to cells far from any surface?
How are gas exchange and cellular metabolism coupled?
How do biological systems shut things down when needed?
How does the environment drive species to diverge?
How do rising CO₂ levels ripple through living systems?
How does energy conversion power what living things do?
What keeps genetic information stable across generations?
Where in biology does direction itself matter?
What's different about interactions within a species vs between species?
How do species avoid competing directly with each other?
Where does the natural end and the artificial begin?
How does timescale change what biology looks like?
What makes biological sampling actually representative?
Where in biology does doubling or halving happen?
Why do organisms need internal distribution systems at all?
Which biological systems are most vulnerable to temperature shifts?
Why do all living cells use the same handful of building blocks?
What makes a biological theory compelling?
What determines whether a species persists or vanishes?
How do species show both continuous and discontinuous variation?

options.why: H-bonds hold the double helix together. Weak but specific.

options.text: Water cohesion pulling xylem sap up
options.why: H-bonds between water molecules transmit tension to the roots.

options.text: Covalent peptide bonds linking amino acids
options.why: Intramolecular covalent bonds, not intermolecular forces.
Nonpolar residues cluster away from water, stabilizing the fold.
Hydrophobic tails hide from water; polar heads face it.

options.text: Electrons shared in a C=C double bond
options.why: A covalent bond within a molecule, not a force between molecules.

options.text: Alveolar gas exchange in the lungs
options.why: Thin moist membrane, huge area. Exchange happens nowhere else.
Epidermal extensions exist purely to maximize absorbing surface.
Happens in cytoplasm. Solution-phase chemistry, no surface involved.
Microvilli amplify the gut lining's surface area enormously.
Light reactions happen at membrane-embedded protein complexes.
Sequential chemistry along an mRNA strand, not an interface process.

options.text: Dandelion seeds carried on the wind
options.why: Parachute fluff carries seeds far from the parent plant.

options.text: Coconut fruits floating to new shores
options.why: Buoyant husks ride ocean currents to colonize islands.

options.text: Kangaroo joey developing in the pouch
options.why: Offspring stays attached to the mother. The opposite of dispersal.
Silk threads catch the wind, scattering young far apart.
Hook-and-loop seeds hitch rides to distant habitats.

options.text: Salmon returning to their natal streams to spawn
options.why: Returning to the parent location. Anti-dispersal, not dispersal.

options.text: Cheetah speed selecting for faster prey
options.why: A locomotor arms race drives morphology in both predator and prey.
Return to water reshaped the entire body plan around swimming.
Corals don't move. No locomotion to contribute to anything.
Powered flight unlocked ecological space no other vertebrate filled.
Walking upright let hands specialize for tools and manipulation.
Passive transport, not self-powered locomotion.

options.text: Enzyme specificity from active site shape
options.why: A component property: shape determines function.
Kinks in lipid chains keep the bilayer fluid at cell temperatures.
An emergent system-level pattern, not a property of a component.
A property of one protein that shapes how oxygen is transported.
Hydrophobic and van der Waals interactions between stacked bases.
A whole-system property, not the property of any component.
Nearly all biomass traces back to captured sunlight.
Human skin needs photons to make an essential vitamin.
They make light, don't depend on it. Keyword trap.
Whole ecosystems thrive in total darkness. Light isn't essential everywhere.
Biological clocks are set by the light-dark cycle.

options.text: Deep-sea anglerfish luring prey with a lure
options.why: The fish generates the light for hunting. Light as tool, not dependency.
One shape fits one substrate. Extreme specificity.
Each antibody binds one molecular target with high precision.
A universal core process. Neither specific nor versatile in the relevant sense.
Versatility lets them exploit shifting resources.
One cell type can become many. Maximum versatility.
An evolved trait, not a specificity-vs-versatility trade-off.
Inputs: light, CO₂, water. Output: glucose and O₂.
External digestion, then absorption of dead matter.
Biosynthesis inside every cell, not a mode of nutrition.
Internal digestion of whole food, with waste as output.
Inorganic chemicals in, organic compounds out.
A cell-division process, not a nutrition mode. No inputs in the relevant sense.
A simplification that captures specificity without molecular detail.
Visualizes proteins floating in a lipid sea. Guides thinking.
This is observation, not modeling. The opposite approach.
Mark-recapture math estimates what can't be counted directly.
An idealized baseline that reveals when evolution is happening.

options.text: A photograph of a whole ecosystem
options.why: A direct record, not a simplified representation. Not a model.
Population can't exceed what the environment supplies.
All active sites occupied. Rate can't go higher.
An encoding property, not a capacity limit.
Four binding sites per molecule. A hard ceiling.
At high light, CO₂ becomes the bottleneck.
A predicted frequency, not a capacity constraint.
High SA:V ratio lets diffusion supply the whole cell.
Large mass, low SA:V. Heat is lost slowly.
Speed comes from limb proportions, not size category.
Small bodies let air tubes reach every cell without lungs.
Only a huge mouth and gut can process enough small prey.

options.text: Octopus camouflaging on the sea floor
options.why: A neural and pigment ability. Unrelated to being big or small.
Chemical concentration tells cells which tissue to become.
Selective transcription produces cell-specific proteins.
Produces identical daughter cells. No differentiation at all.
Random silencing creates differentiated cell lineages.
External cues push uncommitted cells toward a fate.
Copies the genome identically. Doesn't drive specialization.
A pump and tubes deliver materials to every tissue.
Parallel transport systems for water and sugars.
Unicellular. No multicellular problem to solve.
Air tubes bring O₂ directly to every cell.
Being thin means no cell is far from the outside.

options.text: A yeast cell budding off a daughter
options.why: Single-celled reproduction. No distribution problem exists.
CO₂ from respiration drives ventilation up to match demand.
Exchange supplies CO₂ to a metabolic pathway in real time.
Transport between exchange and metabolism. Is neither.
Exchange surface directly feeds cellular respiration.

options.text: Bohr shift releasing O₂ to active tissues
options.why: Metabolic CO₂ triggers extra O₂ delivery where it's needed.
Intracellular storage, not exchange or metabolism itself.
Blocking the active site prevents substrate binding.
End product shuts off the enzyme that starts the pathway.
This is activation, not inhibition. The opposite.
Inhibitory neurotransmitter keeps circuits in check.
They stop cell division when something is wrong.

options.text: Signal amplification in a kinase cascade
options.why: Amplifies the signal rather than inhibiting anything.
Different islands pushed beak shapes apart.
New habitats and niches drove rapid divergence.
Genetic sameness. The opposite of diversification.
Isolation let many species emerge from one.
A new cultural environment selected for a new allele.

options.text: Cloning a sheep from a somatic cell
options.why: Deliberate genetic duplication. No divergence involved.
Dissolved CO₂ forms carbonic acid, weakening skeletons.
CO₂ is the dominant long-lived greenhouse gas.
Different molecules, different problem. Atmospheric but unrelated.

options.text: Plant C:N ratio shifting with CO₂
options.why: More carbon in tissues means less nitrogen per bite for herbivores.
Warming redraws where organisms can live.
A pollution issue, but not connected to CO₂ levels.
Electromagnetic energy becomes chemical bonds.
Chemical energy in phosphate bonds becomes mechanical work.
Passive movement down a gradient. No energy transformed.
Proton gradient energy is captured in ATP's bonds.
Chemical energy becomes visible photons.

options.text: Diffusion of oxygen into a cell
options.why: Passive down-gradient movement. No conversion.
Each new strand templated from an original. Sequence preserved.
Halve-then-restore keeps chromosome number stable across generations.
Generates variation. The opposite of continuity.
Error-checking keeps mutation rates extremely low.
Every daughter cell inherits the full genome unchanged.
Shuffles parental chromosomes. Generates variation, not continuity.
RNA polymerase only adds at the 3' end. Direction is built in.

options.text: Phloem flow from source to sink
options.why: Sugars flow only from where they're made to where they're used.
Direction depends entirely on the gradient. No inherent direction.
The refractory period forces the signal to travel one way.

options.text: Peristalsis moving food through the gut
options.why: Muscular waves push contents in one direction only.

options.text: Heat loss from a warm body to cold air
options.why: Direction set by temperature difference, not by the biology itself.
Intraspecific cooperation. Same species working as one.

options.text: Lions and hyenas fighting over carrion
options.why: Classic interspecific competition at a kill.
No interaction between organisms at all. Wrong scale.
Intraspecific competition for reproduction.
Interspecific mutualism. Different species exchanging resources.
A molecular event, not an interaction between organisms.
Resource partitioning lets coexisting species avoid overlap.
Coexisting species evolve different beaks to use different seeds.
Cooperation between species. Not a competition mechanism at all.
Temporal partitioning splits the day between competitors.

options.text: Savanna grazers at different grass heights
options.why: Stratified feeding divides the same plants into separate niches.
An across-trophic-level interaction, not competition between competitors.
Humans chose the traits. Deliberate, artificial selection.
No human hand. Pure natural selection.

options.text: Mitosis in an onion root tip
options.why: A neutral biological process. Doesn't engage the distinction.
Artificial process reshaping what evolution took millennia to do.
Human design inserted into a living system.

options.text: Bacteria evolving antibiotic resistance in a hospital
options.why: Natural selection, even though the pressure is human-caused.
E. coli can divide every 20 minutes in good conditions.
Action potentials travel and reset almost instantly.
A number, not a timescale. Not even time.
Speciation typically unfolds across deep time.

options.text: Seasonal leaf fall in deciduous trees
options.why: An annual cycle tied to day length and temperature.
A size, not a duration. Wrong kind of quantity.
Randomness avoids bias in estimating plant cover.
Sampling lets you infer population size without counting everyone.

options.text: Counting every tree in a forest
options.why: A census, not sampling. The opposite approach.
Multiple samples reveal how representative any one is.
Systematic sampling captures how communities change with conditions.
Biased selection. Violates the whole point of sampling.
Every chromosome becomes two sister chromatids. Exact doubling.
2n becomes n. Halving is the defining feature.
Radioactive decay is physical, not biological.
One cell doubles its contents, then splits in two.
Under ideal conditions, populations double at a fixed interval.
Amino acids link sequentially. No doubling or halving involved.
Mitochondria in every tissue need constant O₂ delivery.
Leaves make more than they use. Growing tissues need the surplus.
A structural boundary, not a distribution system.
Photosynthesis and cooling both depend on root-sourced water.
Without return flow, tissues swell and circulation fails.
Storage, not distribution. Materials don't leave for other cells.
Heat breaks the bonds holding the active site in shape.

options.text: Reptile sex determined by egg temperature
options.why: Incubation warmth decides male or female.
Set by alleles at conception. Temperature has no effect.
Warm water expels the symbiotic algae corals depend on.
Lipids stiffen when cold and loosen when warm.
A genetic trait. Temperature plays no role.
Every known cell uses ATP. Bacteria, plants, animals, fungi.
All cellular life stores its information in DNA.
Only fungi and arthropods. Not universal at all.

options.text: The same 20 amino acids in proteins
options.why: Nearly every organism builds proteins from the same alphabet.
Universal architecture for separating inside from outside.
A useful molecule but restricted to certain lineages. Not universal.
Explains diverse phenomena from one simple mechanism.
All life is cells. Sweeping, testable, predictive.

options.text: A catalogue of every known beetle
options.why: A list, not a theory. No explanatory power.
Multiple independent lines of evidence converge on one story.
The structure itself suggested how copying must work.

options.text: A single anecdote about an unusual animal
options.why: One observation, not a framework for understanding.
A naive island species couldn't cope with new threats.
Persistence through adaptation to a changing environment.
Organism-level mortality. Doesn't determine whether a species persists.
A lineage surviving almost unchanged across deep time.

options.text: Passenger pigeon wiped out by hunting
options.why: Sudden human pressure collapsed a once-vast population.

options.text: A zoo population growing in captivity
options.why: An individual collection, not a measure of species survival in the wild.

options.text: Human height as a continuous trait
options.why: Many genes and environment combine. Any value possible.

options.text: ABO blood groups as discrete categories
options.why: A single gene, four phenotypes, no in-between.
Genetic sameness shows neither kind of variation.
Multiple loci add up to a smooth range of shades.

options.text: Mendel's pea shape. Round or wrinkled
options.why: One gene, two sharp categories, classic discontinuous variation.
Loss of a population, not a pattern of variation within one.
